entries.definition:
كتاب الهمزة

أهر - أهل

(أهل) الهمزة والهاء واللام أصلان متباعدان، أحدهما الأَهْل. قال الخليل: أهل الرجل زَوْجُه. والتأهُّل التّزَوّج. وأهْل الرّجُل أخصُّ النّاسِ به. وأهل البيت: سُكّانه. وأهل الإسلام: مَن يَدِينُ به. وجميع الأهل أَهْلُون. والأهالي جماعةُ الجماعة. قال النابغة :

ثلاثَةَ أهْلِينَ أفْنَيْتُهُمْ

وكان الإلهُ هو المُسْتآسا

وتقول: أهّلْتُه لهذا الأمر تأهيلاً. ومكان آهِلٌ مَأْهول. قال:

وقِدْماً كانَ مَأْهولاً

فأَمْسَى مرتَعَ العُفْرِ

وقال الراجز :

عرَفْتُ بالنَّصرية المنازلا

قفراً وكانت مِنْهُمُ مآهِلاَ

وكلُّ شيءٍ من الدوابّ وغيرها إذا ألف مكاناً فهو آهِلٌ وأهْلِيٌّ. وفي الحديث:

ʾ–h–l family, kin; to be populated; to be tamed; to be worthy, to be deserving; to take a wife. Of this root, أَهْل ʾahl occurs 127 times.
أَهْل ʾahl [collect. n.] 1 people (2:126) رَبِّ ٱجْعَلْ هَٰذَا بَلَدًا ءَامِنًا وَٱرْزُقْ أَهْلَهُ مِنَ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ مَنْ ءَامَنَ مِنْهُم بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْءَاخِرِ my Lord, make this land secure and provide with produce those among its people who believe in God and the Last Day 2 family, household (28:12) هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ يَكْفُلُونَهُ لَكُمْ shall I guide you to a household who could rear him for you? 3 euphemism for wife (12:25) قَالَتْ مَا جَزَآءُ مَنْ أَرَادَ بِأَهْلِكَ سُوٓءًا she said, ‘What punishment befits the one who intended harm to your wife?’ 4 owners (4:58) إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَن تُؤَدُّوا۟ ٱلْأَمَٰنَٰتِ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهَا God commands you to return trusts to their owners 5 dwellers, inhabitants, occupants (18:71) أَخَرَقْتَهَا لِتُغْرِقَ أَهْلَهَا لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْـًٔا إِمْرًا did you make a hole in it, so as to drown its passengers [occupants]?, indeed, you have done a foul thing! 6 [as first part in construct (إضافة)] ‘worthy of’, ‘deserving of’ (74:56) هُوَ أَهْلُ ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ He is most deserving of being heeded; *(5:47) أَهْلُ ٱلْإِنجِيلِ the people of the Gospel, Christians; *(33:33) أَهْلَ ٱلْبَيْتِ people of the House, the Prophet’s household; also used in specific reference to members of Prophet Abraham’s household in verse (11:73) رَحْمَتُ ٱللَّهِ وَبَرَكَٰتُهُۥ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ ٱلْبَيْتِ the Mercy and Blessings of God [are] upon you people of the household [of Abraham]; *(16:43) أَهْلَ ٱلذِّكْرِ those who have the knowledge; or those who have had [previous] revelations, i.e. Jews and Christians; *(3:64) أَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَٰبِ people of the Book, Jews in particular, and Christians; *(38:64) أَهْلَ ٱلنَّارِ inhabitants of Hellfire.

آل ʾāl (see أ/و/ل ʾ–w–l).

أَو ʾaw conjunction (حرف عطف) occurring some 280 times in the Qurʾan and conveying various specific meanings including the following: 1 doubt (الشك) (18:19) لَبِثْنَا يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ we have remained a day or part of a day 2 vagueness (الإبهام) (34:24) وَإِنَّآ أَوْ إِيَّاكُمْ لَعَلَىٰ هُدًى أَوْ فِى ضَلَٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ and indeed either we or you are following the right guidance or are in clear error 3 giving

62

أَو

options (التخيير), ‘either … or’ and, after prohibitory لا forbidding all options and choices, ‘neither … nor’ (76:24) لَا تُطِعْ مِنْهُمْ ءَاثِمًا أَوْ كَفُورًا do not obey any sinner or disbeliever among them 4 allowing a thing or making it allowable (الإباحة) (24:61) أَن تَأْكُلُوا۟ مِنۢ بُيُوتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ ءَابَآئِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أُمَّهَٰتِكُمْ that you eat from your houses, or the houses of your fathers, or the houses of your mothers 5 unrestricted conjunction (مطلق الجمع), as in the meaning of ‘and’ (معنى الواو) (11:87) أَصَلَوٰتُكَ تَأْمُرُكَ أَن نَّتْرُكَ مَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَآ أَوْ أَن نَّفْعَلَ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِنَا مَا نَشَٰٓؤُا۟ does your prayer [religion] tell you to forsake what our forefathers worshipped and refrain from doing whatever we please with our properties? 6 division or separation (التقسيم) (2:135) وَقَالُوا۟ كُونُوا۟ هُودًا أَوْ نَصَٰرَىٰ تَهْتَدُوا۟ they say, ‘Become Jews or Christians, and you will be rightly guided’ 7 transition in the sense of adversative bal (الإضراب معنى بل), ‘nay … rather …’ (2:74) ثُمَّ قَسَتْ قُلُوبُكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ فَهِىَ كَٱلْحِجَارَةِ أَوْ أَشَدُّ قَسْوَةً even after that, your hearts hardened so they were like rocks, or even harder 8 the meaning of exceptive ʾillā (معنى الا) ‘except’, ‘unless’ according to the views of some eminent scholars in interpreting verse (2:236) لَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِن طَلَّقْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ مَا لَمْ تَمَسُّوهُنَّ أَوْ تَفْرِضُوا۟ لَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً there will be no financial liabilities on you [also interpreted as: no blame attached to you] if you divorce women, so long as you have not yet consummated the marriage, unless you have fixed a dower for them.

أَو ʾaw as a rule أَو joins similarly structured units (phrases, clauses, nominal sentences, verbal sentence, etc.) as in the examples quoted above. Deviation from this norm imparts special meaning/importance to the unit which does not structurally agree with the rest in a succession of ʾaw-joined units. An example of this is the particularising of sending a messenger as a means of communication between mortals and God by the use of the verbal clause يُرْسِلَ رَسُولًا in a sequence of non-verbal clauses all of which are joined by أَو in the following verse (42:51) وَمَا كَانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَن يُكَلِّمَهُ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا وَحْيًا أَوْ مِن وَرَآئِ حِجَابٍ أَوْ يُرْسِلَ رَسُولًا فَيُوحِىَ بِإِذْنِهِۦ مَا يَشَآءُ it is not [possible] for a mortal that God speak to him except through revelation or from behind a veil, or if He sends a messenger to reveal, or sends a messenger so he reveals by His leave whatever He wills.

63

62

أَو

options (التخيير), ‘either … or’ and, after prohibitory لا forbidding all options and choices, ‘neither … nor’ (76:24) لَا تُطِعْ مِنْهُمْ ءَاثِمًا أَوْ كَفُورًا do not obey any sinner or disbeliever among them 4 allowing a thing or making it allowable (الإباحة) (24:61) أَن تَأْكُلُوا۟ مِنۢ بُيُوتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ ءَابَآئِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أُمَّهَٰتِكُمْ that you eat from your houses, or the houses of your fathers, or the houses of your mothers 5 unrestricted conjunction (مطلق الجمع), as in the meaning of ‘and’ (معنى الواو) (11:87) أَصَلَوٰتُكَ تَأْمُرُكَ أَن نَّتْرُكَ مَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَآ أَوْ أَن نَّفْعَلَ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِنَا مَا نَشَٰٓؤُا۟ does your prayer [religion] tell you to forsake what our forefathers worshipped and refrain from doing whatever we please with our properties? 6 division or separation (التقسيم) (2:135) وَقَالُوا۟ كُونُوا۟ هُودًا أَوْ نَصَٰرَىٰ تَهْتَدُوا۟ they say, ‘Become Jews or Christians, and you will be rightly guided’ 7 transition in the sense of adversative bal (الإضراب معنى بل), ‘nay … rather …’ (2:74) ثُمَّ قَسَتْ قُلُوبُكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ فَهِىَ كَٱلْحِجَارَةِ أَوْ أَشَدُّ قَسْوَةً even after that, your hearts hardened so they were like rocks, or even harder 8 the meaning of exceptive ʾillā (معنى الا) ‘except’, ‘unless’ according to the views of some eminent scholars in interpreting verse (2:236) لَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِن طَلَّقْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ مَا لَمْ تَمَسُّوهُنَّ أَوْ تَفْرِضُوا۟ لَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً there will be no financial liabilities on you [also interpreted as: no blame attached to you] if you divorce women, so long as you have not yet consummated the marriage, unless you have fixed a dower for them.
